```{r}
  loghsCRP ~ cb.bmi.18 + sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  data = df.dlnm.18.win
)

pred.cb.bmi.18 <- crosspred(cb.bmi.18, dlnm.fit, at = 20:35, bylag = 1, cen = 25, cumul = TRUE)

base_age <- 18

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s11-3d-unpenalized
#| fig-cap: "Three-dimensional exposure-lag-response surface restricted to adult BMI (ages 18--56) with Winsorized values, modeled using AIC-selected natural cubic splines, centered at BMI = 25."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 5
x <- pred.cb.bmi.18$predvar           # BMI (exposure)
y <- (0:(ncol(pred.cb.bmi.18$matfit)-1)) + base_age  # age at exposure
# Transpose matfit to match dimensions: rows = lag, columns = BMI
z <- t(pred.cb.bmi.18$matfit)  # Now: 51 rows (lags) × 41 columns (BMI)

# Create 3D surface plot
plot_ly(
  x = ~x,
  y = ~y,
  z = ~z,
  type = "surface",
  colorscale = "Viridis",
  showscale = FALSE
) %>%
  layout(
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "BMI", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      yaxis = list(title = "Age at Exposure", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      zaxis = list(title = "Effect on log(hsCRP)", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = 1.5, z = 1.5)
      )
    ),
    title = "Exposure–Lag–Response Surface"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s11-dlnm-18-unrestricted
#| fig-cap: "DLNM results restricted to adult BMI (18--56) with natural cubic splines: exposure-response, lag-response, and cumulative effect panels."
#| fig-height: 7

plot_dlnm(pred.cb.bmi.18,
            base_age = 18,
            exposure_vals = c(20, 25, 30, 35),
            ages_to_show = c(20, 30, 40, 50),
            outcome_label = "log(hsCRP)",
            exposure_label = "BMI")
```

### Penalized DLNM with P-splines in both dimensions

```{r}
cb.bmi.18 <- crossbasis(df.dlnm.18.win$pred_bmi, 
                         lag = 39,
                         argvar = list(fun = "ps", df = 6),
                         arglag = list(fun = "ps", df = 13))


cb.bmi.pen.18 <- cbPen(cb.bmi.18)

dlnm.gam <- gam(
  loghsCRP ~ cb.bmi.18 + sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  paraPen = list(cb.bmi = cb.bmi.pen.18),
  method = "GCV.Cp",
  data = df.dlnm.18.win
)

pred.cb.bmi.pen.18 <- crosspred(cb.bmi.18, dlnm.gam, at = 20:35, bylag = 1, cen = 25, cumul = TRUE)

base_age <- 18

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s12-3d-penalized
#| fig-cap: "Three-dimensional exposure-lag-response surface for adult BMI (18--56) using penalized P-splines with GCV-based smoothness selection, centered at BMI = 25."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 5
x <- pred.cb.bmi.pen.18$predvar           # BMI (exposure)
y <- (0:(ncol(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18$matfit)-1)) + base_age  # age at exposure
# Transpose matfit to match dimensions: rows = lag, columns = BMI
z <- t(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18$matfit)  # Now: 51 rows (lags) × 41 columns (BMI)

# Create 3D surface plot
plot_ly(
  x = ~x,
  y = ~y,
  z = ~z,
  type = "surface",
  colorscale = "Viridis",
  showscale = FALSE
) %>%
  layout(
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "BMI", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      yaxis = list(title = "Age at Exposure", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      zaxis = list(title = "Effect on log(hsCRP)", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = 1.5, z = 1.5)
      )
    ),
    title = "Exposure–Lag–Response Surface"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s13-dlnm-18-penalized
#| fig-cap: "DLNM results for adult BMI (18--56) with penalized P-splines: exposure-response, lag-response, and cumulative effect panels."
#| fig-height: 7

plot_dlnm(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18,
            base_age = 18,
            exposure_vals = c(20, 25, 30, 35),
            ages_to_show = c(20, 30, 40, 50),
            outcome_label = "log(hsCRP)",
            exposure_label = "BMI")
```

### Lower Degrees of Freedom

```{r}

cb.bmi.18.lower <- crossbasis(df.dlnm.18.win$pred_bmi, 
                         lag = 39,
                         argvar = list(fun = "ps", df = 6),
                         arglag = list(fun = "ps", df = 8))

cb.bmi.pen.18.lower <- cbPen(cb.bmi.18.lower)

dlnm.gam <- gam(
  loghsCRP ~ cb.bmi.18.lower + sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  paraPen = list(cb.bmi = cb.bmi.pen.18.lower),
  method = "GCV.Cp",
  data = df.dlnm.18.win
)

pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower <- crosspred(cb.bmi.18.lower, dlnm.gam, at = 20:35, bylag = 1, cen = 25, cumul = TRUE)

base_age <- 18

# Save the final chosen model
saveRDS(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower, file = here("Data", "Models", "model.dlnm.rds"))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s14-3d-lower
#| fig-cap: "Three-dimensional exposure-lag-response surface for adult BMI (18--56) using penalized P-splines with reduced lag degrees of freedom (df.lag = 8) to produce a smoother, more interpretable surface."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 5
x <- pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower$predvar           # BMI (exposure)
y <- (0:(ncol(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower$matfit)-1)) + base_age  # age at exposure
# Transpose matfit to match dimensions: rows = lag, columns = BMI
z <- t(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower$matfit)  # Now: 51 rows (lags) × 41 columns (BMI)

# Create 3D surface plot
plot_ly(
  x = ~x,
  y = ~y,
  z = ~z,
  type = "surface",
  colorscale = "Viridis",
  showscale = FALSE
) %>%
  layout(
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "BMI", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      yaxis = list(title = "Age at Exposure", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      zaxis = list(title = "Effect on log(hsCRP)", showgrid = TRUE, gridwidth = 1),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = 1.5, z = 1.5)
      )
    ),
    title = "Exposure–Lag–Response Surface"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s15-dlnm-18-lower
#| fig-cap: "DLNM results for adult BMI (18--56) with lower degrees of freedom penalized P-splines (final selected specification): exposure-response, lag-response, and cumulative effect panels."
#| fig-height: 7

plot_dlnm(pred.cb.bmi.pen.18.lower,
            base_age = 18,
            exposure_vals = c(20, 25, 30, 35),
            ages_to_show = c(20, 30, 40, 50),
            outcome_label = "log(hsCRP)",
            exposure_label = "BMI")
```

<hr style="border: 2px solid black;">

# Structured Life-Course Modeling Approach

The Structured Life Course Modeling Approach (SLCMA) is a hypothesis-driven framework that operationalizes competing life course theories (e.g., accumulation, sensitive periods, and mobility or change) as alternative exposure variables and uses variable selection to identify which hypothesis best explains variation in the outcome.[@mishra2009; @smith2022; @zhu2020] For example, an accumulation of risk hypothesis was represented by total cumulative BMI across the full age range, while sensitive period hypotheses were represented by period-specific mean BMI. The full hypothesis set led to model collinearity; following recommended practice, a reduced model was fit. The mobility/change hypothesis represents the idea that dynamic shifts in adiposity across adulthood may influence later-life inflammation. Models were fit using the `slcma` [@thedunnlab2025] and `lars` [@efron2022] packages in R. To obtain valid inference accounting for the data-driven hypothesis selection process, post-selection inference was conducted using selective inference.

@tbl-s12-hypotheses defines each hypothesis, its algebraic formulation, and a plausible substantive example for the BMI--hsCRP relationship. @tbl-s9-slcma-vars summarizes the distributions of all derived exposure variables, and @fig-s17-corr displays their pairwise correlations.

Variable selection proceeds in two stages. In Stage 1, least angle regression (LARS) is applied to identify the subset of life course variables that best predict log(hsCRP) after adjusting for covariates. The LARS procedure is first applied to all 10 hypothesis-derived variables (@tbl-s10-lars-full), and inference for the fully saturated model is presented in @tbl-s11-slcma-full. A reduced model retaining only the 3 critical period and mobility variables is then fit (@tbl-s13-lars-reduced). In Stage 2, three inference approaches are compared for the reduced model: naive OLS, Bonferroni-corrected OLS, and selective inference based on the fixed lasso (@tbl-s14-slcma-reduced).

## Life Course Hypotheses for BMI Effects on Adult hsCRP

| Hypothesis | Definition | SLCMA Encoding | Algebraic Formulation | Plausible Example for BMI $\rightarrow$ hsCRP |
|------------|------------|----------------|----------------------|-------------------------------------|
| **Critical Period** | Exposure at a specific age/stage has the strongest effect on the outcome| Mean BMI within defined age windows (e.g., childhood, early midlife, late midlife) | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_j X_j$ |**Childhood BMI**: BMI during childhood/adolescence (ages 7-18) has the strongest effect on adult hsCRP due to inflammation during formational growth stage |
| **Accumulation** | Every additional time point of exposure affects the outcome in a dose-response manner, regardless of timing | Sum of BMI values across all ages/stages | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1(\sum_j X_j)$ |**Cumulative inflammatory burden**: Each year spent with high BMI adds to lifetime inflammatory load, with total burden across childhood, adolescence, and adulthood determining adult hsCRP levels |
| **Recency** | More proximal exposures (closer in time to outcome) have stronger effects than distal exposures | Age-weighted sum of BMI (more weight to recent measures) | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1(\sum_j w_j X_j)$ where $w_j$ = age at time $j$ |**Proximal effects**: Late-life BMI has stronger influence on hsCRP than childhood BMI because current adipose tissue mass directly produces inflammatory cytokines |
| **Mobility/Change** | Changes in BMI matter more than absolute levels| Change scores between periods (e.g., BMI gain in the last 10 years) or trajectory indicators | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1(X_j - X_{j-1})$ |**Weight gain trajectory**: Rapid BMI increase in adulthood (upward mobility) triggers greater inflammatory response than stable obesity |
| **Effect Modification** | BMI at one life stage modifies the effect of BMI at another stage; interaction between time periods | Product terms between BMI at different ages/stages (e.g., BMI_childhood $\times$ BMI_adulthood) | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_1 + \beta_2 X_2 + \beta_3(X_1 \times X_2)$ |**Priming**: High childhood BMI "primes" the inflammatory system, making individuals more sensitive to adult BMI effects (childhood obesity + adult obesity leads to multiplicative hsCRP increase) |
| **Threshold** | Only exposure above (or below) a certain threshold affects the outcome; non-linear relationship | Binary indicators for exceeding BMI thresholds (e.g., BMI $\geq 25$ or $\geq 30$) at each time point | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 I(X_j > \tau)$ |**Obesity threshold**: Only BMI $\geq 30$ at any point triggers persistent inflammatory changes; being overweight (BMI 25-29.9) has no lasting effect on chronic inflammation |
| **Accumulation + Threshold** | Total duration of exposure above a threshold affects the outcome in a dose-response manner | Count of time periods where BMI exceeds a threshold (e.g., number of ages where BMI $\geq 30$) | $Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1(\sum_j I(X_j > \tau))$ |**Duration of obesity**: The number of life stages spent with BMI $\geq 30$ determines adult hsCRP; spending 3 periods obese has a greater inflammatory effect than 1 period, regardless of which specific periods |

: Life Course Hypotheses for BMI Effects on Adult hsCRP {#tbl-s12-hypotheses}

*Notation Key:*

- **Y** = hsCRP (outcome)
- **Xⱼ** = BMI at time period/age j
- **β₀** = intercept
- **βⱼ** = regression coefficient for period/age j
- **∑ⱼ** = sum across all time periods
- **I()** = indicator function (1 if true, 0 if false)
- **wⱼ** = weight for period j
- **D⁺ⱼ,ⱼ₊₁** = upward transition from period j to j+1
- **D⁻ⱼ,ⱼ₊₁** = downward transition from period j to j+1
- **τ** = threshold value (e.g., BMI = 30)

## Distributions of Variables from each Hypothesis

```{r}
# Define the SCLMA variables based on the chosen hypotheses and merge with the main dataset

df.slcma <- df.bmi_prediction %>%
  group_by(ncdsid) %>%
  select(ncdsid, xage, pred_bmi, bmi_category) %>%
  summarise(
# Threshold + Accumulation
    yrs_normal      = sum(bmi_category == "Normal", na.rm = TRUE),
    yrs_overweight  = sum(bmi_category == "Overweight", na.rm = TRUE),
    yrs_obese       = sum(bmi_category == "Obese", na.rm = TRUE),
# Critical Period
    mean_bmi_early_life = mean(pred_bmi[xage >= 7 & xage <= 25], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_bmi_early_midlife = mean(pred_bmi[xage > 25 & xage <= 40], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_bmi_late_midlife = mean(pred_bmi[xage > 40 & xage <= 60], na.rm = TRUE),
# Accumulation
    total_bmi = sum(pred_bmi, na.rm = TRUE),
# Recency
    weighted_bmi = sum(pred_bmi * xage, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(xage, na.rm = TRUE),
# Mobility/Change
    bmi_change_late_midlife = mean_bmi_late_midlife - mean_bmi_early_midlife,
# Effect Modification
    bmi_interaction = mean_bmi_early_life * mean_bmi_late_midlife
  ) %>% 
  right_join(df.model, by = "ncdsid")

```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s9-slcma-vars
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive statistics for all SLCMA life course exposure variables, organized by hypothesis (threshold + accumulation, critical period, accumulation, recency, mobility/change, effect modification)."
df.slcma %>%
  select(yrs_normal:bmi_interaction) %>%
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
              label = list(
                yrs_normal ~ "Years with Normal BMI",
                yrs_overweight ~ "Years with Overweight BMI", 
                yrs_obese ~ "Years with Obese BMI",
                total_bmi ~ "Total BMI Score",
                mean_bmi_early_life ~ "Mean BMI Early Life",
                mean_bmi_early_midlife ~ "Mean BMI Early Midlife",
                mean_bmi_late_midlife ~ "Mean BMI Late Midlife",
                weighted_bmi ~ "Weighted BMI",
                bmi_change_late_midlife ~ "BMI Change Late Midlife",
                bmi_interaction ~ "BMI Interaction (Avg Early x Avg Late Midlife)"
              )) %>%
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x %>%
      add_row(label = "    Threshold + Accumulation", .before = 1) %>%
      add_row(label = "    Critical Period", .before = 5) %>%
      add_row(label = "    Accumulation", .before = 9) %>%
      add_row(label = "    Recency", .before = 11) %>%
      add_row(label = "    Mobility/Change", .before = 13) %>%
      add_row(label = "    Effect Modification", .before = 15)
  ) %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_fill(color = "#f0f0f0"),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = label %in% c("    Threshold + Accumulation", "    Critical Period", "    Accumulation", "    Recency", "    Mobility/Change", "    Effect Modification")
    )
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-s16-slcma-hist
#| fig-cap: "Histograms showing the distribution of each SLCMA life course exposure variable."

# Prepare data for plotting
plot_data <- df.slcma %>%
    select(yrs_normal:bmi_interaction) %>%
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
    mutate(
      category = case_when(
        variable %in% c("yrs_normal", "yrs_overweight", "yrs_obese", "total_bmi") ~ "1. Threshold + Accumulation",
        variable %in% c("mean_bmi_early_life", "mean_bmi_early_midlife", "mean_bmi_late_midlife") ~ "2. Critical Period",
        variable == "weighted_bmi" ~ "3. Recency",
        variable == "bmi_change_late_midlife" ~ "4. Mobility/Change",
        variable == "bmi_interaction" ~ "5. Effect Modification"
      ),
      variable_label = case_when(
        variable == "yrs_normal" ~ "Years Normal BMI",
        variable == "yrs_overweight" ~ "Years Overweight BMI",
        variable == "yrs_obese" ~ "Years Obese BMI",
        variable == "mean_bmi_early_life" ~ "Mean BMI (7-25)",
        variable == "mean_bmi_early_midlife" ~ "Mean BMI (26-40)",
        variable == "mean_bmi_late_midlife" ~ "Mean BMI (41-56)",
        variable == "total_bmi" ~ "Total BMI Score",
        variable == "weighted_bmi" ~ "Weighted BMI",
        variable == "bmi_change_late_midlife" ~ "BMI Change (Late Midlife)",
        variable == "bmi_interaction" ~ "BMI Interaction"
      ),
      # Order by category then within-category logic
      variable_label = factor(variable_label, levels = c(
        # Threshold + Accumulation
        "Years Normal BMI",
        "Years Overweight BMI",
        "Years Obese BMI",
        # Critical Period (chronological)
        "Mean BMI (7-25)",
        "Mean BMI (26-40)",
        "Mean BMI (41-56)",
        # Accumulation
        "Total BMI Score",
        # Recency
        "Weighted BMI",
        # Mobility
        "BMI Change (Late Midlife)",
        # Effect Modification
        "BMI Interaction"
      ))
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value))

  # Use category as row facet for clearer grouping
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = value)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~ variable_label, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    labs(
      title = "Distribution of BMI Exposure Variables",
      x = "Value",
      y = "Frequency"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
```

## Correlation Matrix for Variables from each Hypothesis

```{r}
#| label: fig-s17-corr
#| fig-cap: "Pairwise Pearson correlation matrix of all SLCMA exposure variables, illustrating the degree of collinearity among life course hypothesis encodings."
library(corrplot)
library(ggcorrplot)

var_labels <- c(
  "yrs_normal" = "Years Normal BMI",
  "yrs_overweight" = "Years Overweight BMI",
  "yrs_obese" = "Years Obese BMI",
  "total_bmi" = "Total BMI Score",
  "mean_bmi_early_life" = "Mean BMI (7-25)",
  "mean_bmi_early_midlife" = "Mean BMI (26-40)",
  "mean_bmi_late_midlife" = "Mean BMI (41-56)",
  "weighted_bmi" = "Weighted BMI",
  "bmi_change_late_midlife" = "BMI Change (Late Midlife)",
  "bmi_interaction" = "BMI Interaction"
)

var_order <- c(
  "yrs_normal", "yrs_overweight", "yrs_obese",
  "mean_bmi_early_life", "mean_bmi_early_midlife", "mean_bmi_late_midlife",
  "total_bmi",
  "weighted_bmi", "bmi_change_late_midlife", "bmi_interaction"
)

cor_matrix <- df.slcma %>%
  select(all_of(var_order)) %>%
  rename_with(~ var_labels[.x], .cols = everything()) %>%
  cor()

ggcorrplot(cor_matrix,
           hc.order = FALSE,
           type = "upper",
           lab = TRUE,
           lab_size = 2,
           colors = c("#6D9EC1", "white", "#E46726"),
           title = "Correlation Matrix of BMI Life Course Variables",
           ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

## All Variables Model

### LARS (Stage 1)

```{r}
#| output: FALSE
library(lars)

mod.slcma <- slcma::slcma(
  loghsCRP ~ yrs_normal + yrs_obese + yrs_overweight + total_bmi + weighted_bmi +
    mean_bmi_early_life + mean_bmi_early_midlife + mean_bmi_late_midlife +
    bmi_interaction + bmi_change_late_midlife +
    sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  adjust = c(11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18),
  data = df.slcma
)

mod.slcma.naive <- lm(
  loghsCRP ~ yrs_normal + yrs_obese + yrs_overweight + total_bmi + weighted_bmi +
    mean_bmi_early_life + mean_bmi_early_midlife + mean_bmi_late_midlife +
    bmi_interaction + bmi_change_late_midlife +
    sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  data = df.slcma
)
```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-s10-lars-full
#| tbl-cap: "LARS variable selection steps for the full SLCMA model with all seleted life course hypothesis variables, showing the order of entry, removals, and cumulative partial R-squared at each step."

summary_obj <- summary(mod.slcma)

lars_summary <- data.frame(
  Step = summary_obj$step,
  `Variable_selected` = summary_obj$selected,
  `Variable_removed` = summary_obj$removed,
  Variables = summary_obj$vars,
  `Partial_R_squared` = summary_obj$fit$R2
)

# Create a formatted table for the LARS procedure
lars_table <- lars_summary %>%
  mutate(
    `Partial R²` = sprintf("%.3f", Partial_R_squared),
    Step = as.character(Step),
    `Variable selected` = Variable_selected,
    `Variable removed` = Variable_removed
  ) %>%
  select(Step, `Variable selected`, `Variable removed`, Variables, `Partial R²`) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "LARS Procedure Summary",
    subtitle = "Variable Selection Steps for BMI → hsCRP Analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c("Step", "Variables", "Partial R²")
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "left", 
    columns = c("Variable selected", "Variable removed")
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "LARS = Least Angle Regression; Variables = number of variables in model",
    locations = cells_title()
  )

lars_table

```


### Inference (Stage 2)

```{r}
library(mvtnorm)

# Define the fixed lasso inference function from the selectiveInference package
fixedLassoInf <- selectiveInference::fixedLassoInf

# Compute selective inference for the full model at 11th step - this should be similar to naive OLS
full_infer <- slcma::slcmaInfer(mod.slcma, 11, method=c("relax"))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s11-slcma-full
#| tbl-cap: "Naive OLS inference results for the full SLCMA model at the final LARS step, reporting estimates and 95% CIs for all 10 life course hypothesis variables without multiple testing correction."

full_infer$relax$coefficients %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Variable") %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = case_when(
      Variable == "yrs_normal" ~ "Years normal weight",
      Variable == "yrs_obese" ~ "Years obese",
      Variable == "yrs_overweight" ~ "Years overweight",
      Variable == "total_bmi" ~ "Total BMI",
      Variable == "weighted_bmi" ~ "Weighted BMI",
      Variable == "mean_bmi_early_life" ~ "Mean BMI (early life)",
      Variable == "mean_bmi_early_midlife" ~ "Mean BMI (early midlife)",
      Variable == "mean_bmi_late_midlife" ~ "Mean BMI (late midlife)",
      Variable == "bmi_interaction" ~ "BMI interaction",
      Variable == "bmi_change_late_midlife" ~ "BMI change (late midlife)",
      TRUE ~ Variable
    ),
    `95% CI` = paste0("(", sprintf("%.3f", CI.lo), ", ", sprintf("%.3f", CI.up), ")")
  ) %>%
  select(Variable, Coef, SE, `95% CI`) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(Coef, SE), decimals = 3) %>%
  cols_label(
    Variable = "Hypothesis",
    Coef = "Estimate",
    SE = "SE"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "SLCMA Full Model without Multiple Testing Correction"
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(13),
    heading.title.font.size = px(16),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold"
  )
```

## Reduced Variables Model

### LARS (Stage 1)

```{r}
#| output: FALSE
library(lars)

mod.slcma.reduced <- slcma::slcma(
  loghsCRP ~ mean_bmi_early_life + mean_bmi_early_midlife + mean_bmi_early_life +
    bmi_change_late_midlife +
    sex + sweep10_age + educ_qual + father_bmi + mother_bmi + n646 + n607 + n2383,
  adjust = c(4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12),
  data = df.slcma
)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s13-lars-reduced
#| tbl-cap: "LARS variable selection steps for the reduced SLCMA model with 3 life course hypothesis variables (critical period and mobility), showing the order of entry and cumulative partial R-squared."
summary_obj <- summary(mod.slcma.reduced) %>% suppressMessages()

# Create LARS summary table
lars_summary <- data.frame(
  Step = summary_obj$step,
  `Variable_selected` = summary_obj$selected,
  `Variable_removed` = summary_obj$removed,
  Variables = summary_obj$vars,
  `Partial_R_squared` = summary_obj$fit$R2
)

# Create a formatted table for the LARS procedure
lars_table <- lars_summary %>%
  mutate(
    `Partial R²` = sprintf("%.3f", Partial_R_squared),
    Step = as.character(Step),
    `Variable selected` = Variable_selected,
    `Variable removed` = Variable_removed
  ) %>%
  select(Step, `Variable selected`, `Variable removed`, Variables, `Partial R²`) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "LARS Procedure Summary",
    subtitle = "Variable Selection Steps for BMI → hsCRP Analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c("Step", "Variables", "Partial R²")
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "left", 
    columns = c("Variable selected", "Variable removed")
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "LARS = Least Angle Regression; Variables = number of variables in model",
    locations = cells_title()
  )

lars_table
```


### Selective Inference (Stage 2)

```{r}
# Compute both fixed lasso and relaxed lasso inference for the reduced model at 3rd step
fixed_lasso <- slcma::slcmaInfer(mod.slcma.reduced, 3, method = "selectiveInference")
relaxed_lasso <- slcma::slcmaInfer(mod.slcma.reduced, 3, method = "relax")

order_vec <- c(
  "bmi_change_late_midlife",
  "mean_bmi_early_life",
  "mean_bmi_early_midlife"
)

npred <- length(order_vec)

# Compute Naive OLS estimates with Bonferroni-corrected CIs and p-values
slcma_table <- relaxed_lasso$relax$coefficients %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Variable") %>%
  filter(Variable %in% order_vec) %>%
  mutate(
    pvalue = 2 * pt(abs(Coef / SE), df = nrow(df.slcma) - npred, lower.tail = FALSE),
    Bonf_pvalue = pmin(pvalue * npred, 1),
    Bonf_CI_lo = Coef + qt(0.025 / npred, df = nrow(df.slcma) - npred) * SE,
    Bonf_CI_up = Coef + qt(1 - 0.025 / npred, df = nrow(df.slcma) - npred) * SE,
    Variable = factor(Variable, levels = order_vec)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Variable)

# Fixed Lasso selective inference results
var_names_fl <- names(fixed_lasso$fli$vars)
fixed_df <- tibble(
  Variable = var_names_fl,
  FL_Estimate = as.numeric(fixed_lasso$fli$coef0),
  FL_pvalue = fixed_lasso$fli$pv,
  FL_CI_lo = fixed_lasso$fli$ci[, 1],
  FL_CI_up = fixed_lasso$fli$ci[, 2]
) %>%
  filter(Variable %in% order_vec) %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = order_vec)) %>%
  arrange(Variable)

# Merge
slcma_table <- slcma_table %>%
  left_join(fixed_df, by = "Variable")

saveRDS(slcma_table, file = here("Data", "Models", "model.slcma.reduced.rds"))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-s14-slcma-reduced
#| tbl-cap: "Comparison of three inference methods (naive OLS, Bonferroni-corrected OLS, and fixed lasso selective inference) for the reduced SLCMA model, reporting estimates, 95% CIs, and p-values for the 3 selected life course variables."
# GT table

slcma_table %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = case_when(
      Variable == "bmi_change_late_midlife" ~ "BMI change (late midlife)",
      Variable == "mean_bmi_early_life" ~ "Mean BMI (early life)",
      Variable == "mean_bmi_early_midlife" ~ "Mean BMI (early midlife)"
    ),
    `95% CI` = paste0("(", sprintf("%.3f", CI.lo), ", ", sprintf("%.3f", CI.up), ")"),
    `Bonf 95% CI` = paste0("(", sprintf("%.3f", Bonf_CI_lo), ", ", sprintf("%.3f", Bonf_CI_up), ")"),
    `SI 95% CI` = paste0("(", sprintf("%.3f", FL_CI_lo), ", ", sprintf("%.3f", FL_CI_up), ")")
  ) %>%
  select(Variable, Coef, SE, `95% CI`, pvalue,
         `Bonf 95% CI`, Bonf_pvalue,
         FL_Estimate, `SI 95% CI`, FL_pvalue) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(Coef, SE, FL_Estimate), decimals = 3) %>%
  fmt(columns = c(pvalue, Bonf_pvalue, FL_pvalue), fns = function(x) {
    ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", x))
  }) %>%
  cols_label(
    Variable = "Exposure",
    Coef = "Estimate",
    SE = "SE",
    pvalue = "p-value",
    Bonf_pvalue = "p-value",
    FL_Estimate = "Estimate",
    FL_pvalue = "p-value"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "SLCMA Inference Methods Comparison",
    subtitle = "Estimates with Bonferroni and Selective Inference corrections"
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Naive OLS", columns = c(Coef, SE, `95% CI`, pvalue)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Bonferroni", columns = c(`Bonf 95% CI`, Bonf_pvalue)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Selective Inference", columns = c(FL_Estimate, `SI 95% CI`, FL_pvalue)) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(13),
    heading.title.font.size = px(16),
    heading.subtitle.font.size = px(13),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold"
  )
```

<hr style="border: 2px solid black;">

# Lifecourse Methods Key Decision Points {.smaller .custom-small}

Each life course method involves a series of analytic decisions that shape the resulting estimates. @tbl-s15-decisions catalogues the key decision points for each method, including choices about exposure operationalization, model specification, basis function selection, clustering criteria, and inference procedures. These decisions represent sources of researcher degrees of freedom that should be reported transparently.

| Method | Key Decision Points |
|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| **Single Numeric Summary** | \- Definition of the exposure window (entire observed life course vs restricted age ranges)<br>- Choice of summary metric (mean BMI, cumulative BMI, maximum BMI, threshold-based summaries) |
| **Time Period Comparison** | \- Definition and number of life course periods and their boundaries<br>- Summary metric within each period (mean BMI, cumulative exposure, or threshold-based measures)<br> - Model specification (conditional inclusion of all periods vs separate period-specific models) |
| **Multiple Informant Model** | \- Definition of time windows<br>- Summary metric within each period <br> - Estimation framework (GEE vs maximum likelihood)<br>- Correlation structure specification (typically independent)<br> - Choice of reference period and contrast coding for hypothesis testing <br> |
| **Sequence Analysis** | \- Number and definition of exposure states <br>- Distance metric for sequence comparison (optimal matching, dynamic Hamming, etc.) <br>- Cost specification for substitutions, insertions, and deletions in optimal matching <br>- Clustering algorithm (hierarchical, PAM, k-means) <br>- Number of clusters to retain and cluster validation criteria <br>- Naming and substantive interpretation of sequence patterns |
| **Latent Class Trajectory Modeling** | \- Number of time points included in trajectory modeling <br>- Functional form of trajectories (linear, quadratic, cubic, piecewise) <br>- Random effects structure (random intercepts, random slopes, both) <br>- Covariance structure for random effects and residuals<br>- Number of latent classes to retain and class enumeration criteria (BIC, AIC, entropy, likelihood ratio tests)<br>- Method for handling class uncertainty (modal assignment, two-step, three-step approaches)<br>- Naming and substantive interpretation of trajectory classes |
| **Distributed Lag Non-linear Models** | \- Exposure window and lag range (e.g., restricting to adult exposures; maximum lag)<br>- Basis function choice for exposure and lag (e.g., natural splines vs P-splines)<br>- Degrees of freedom and knot placement strategy<br>- Assessment of model fit and model selection criterion <br>- Penalized vs unpenalized estimation and smoothness selection e.g., GCV <br>- Exposure centering value for interpretation (e.g., BMI = 25) <br>- Choice of summaries and visualizations (exposure–lag surface, lag slices, exposure slices, cumulative effects) |
| **Structured Life Course Modeling Approach** | \- Life course hypotheses to encode <br>- Operationalization of hypotheses as exposure variables<br>- Variable selection procedure (LARS/forward selection)<br>- Criteria for reduced model specification under collinearity<br>- Selective inference method <br>- Interpretation of selected variables as dominant life course mechanisms|

: Life Course Methods: Key Decision Points {#tbl-s15-decisions}

<hr style="border: 2px solid black;">
